memoryArtifacts.headline: Robert II d'Artois (1250-1302)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Robert II d'Artois, (septembre 1250 - 11 juillet 1302), fils posthume de Robert Ier et de Mathilde de Brabant, devient dès sa naissance comte d'Artois.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois於1250年9月17日在法國受洗時，他的父親Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois33歲，母親Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant26歲。他於1262年6月13日與Amicie de Courtenay在法國瑟-吉馬內瑟-波特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1302年7月11日在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫可特利可享年51歲，埋葬在法國瑟-吉瓦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois于1250年9月17日在法国受洗时，他的父亲Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois33岁，母亲Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant26岁。他于1262年6月13日与Amicie de Courtenay在法国瑟-吉馬內瑟-波特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1302年7月11日在比利时維斯吉福拉安德倫可特利可享年51岁，埋葬在法国瑟-吉瓦。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: "le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

nameMeanings.name: Noble"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: name derived from the modern English adjective (via Old French from Latin nobilis). The idea behind it may have been to imply aristocratic origin or to suggest qualities of character. In part there may be some influence from the surname, which arose in the Middle Ages from a descriptive nickname in the first sense.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois was christened on 17 September 1250, in France, his father, Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois, was 33 and his mother, Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant, was 26. He married Amicie de Courtenay on 13 June 1262, in Seine-Port, Seine-et-Marne, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 11 July 1302, in Kortrijk, West Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 51, and was buried in Seine-et-Oise, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois a été baptisé le 17 septembre 1250, à France, son père, Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Amicie de Courtenay le 13 juin 1262, à Seine-Port, Seine-et-Marne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 11 juillet 1302, à Kortrijk, Flandre occidentale, Belgique, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Seine-et-Oise, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois am 17. September 1250 in Frankreich getauft wurde, war sein Vater Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois 33 und seine Mutter Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant 26. Er hat am 13. Juni 1262 in Seine-Port, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich Amicie de Courtenay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 11. Juli 1302 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Kortrijk, West-Flandern, Belgien und wurde in Seine-et-Oise, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois è stato battezzato il 17 settembre 1250 nel Francia, il suo padre, Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Amicie de Courtenay il 13 giugno 1262, nel Seine-Port, Seine-et-Marne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 11 luglio 1302, nel Kortrijk, Fiandra Occidentale, Belgio, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Seine-et-Oise, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artoisが1250年9月17日にフランスで洗礼を受けた時，父Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artoisは33歳，母Matilda, countess of artois of Brabantは26歳であった。1262年6月13日にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンセアイネ-プオルトでAmicie de Courtenayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1302年7月11日にベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンコルトリジクで51歳で死去し，フランスセアイネ-エト-オイゼにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois(이)가 1250년 9월 17일 프랑스에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1262년 6월 13일 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 세이인-포트에서 Amicie de Courtenay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1302년 7월 11일 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 코르트리제이크에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세이인-트-이스에 묻혔다.

mn.17 September 1250–11 July 1302: 1250 оны Есдүгээр сарын 17–1302 оны Долоодугаар сарын 11

pt.17 September 1250–11 July 1302: 17 de setembro de 1250–11 de julho de 1302
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois foi batizado em 17 de setembro de 1250, em França, seu pai, Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Amicie de Courtenay em 13 de junho de 1262, em Seine-Port, Seine-et-Marne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 11 de julho de 1302, em Kortrijk, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Seine-et-Oise, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois был крещен 17 сентября 1250 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Amicie de Courtenay 13 июня 1262 г. в местности сине-порт, сине-ет-марн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 11 июля 1302 г. в местности кортриджк, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности сине-ет-оис, Франция.

es.17 September 1250–11 July 1302: 17 de septiembre de 1250–11 de julio de 1302
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert II "le Noble" Comte d'Artois se bautizó el 17 de septiembre de 1250, en Francia, su papá, Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Amicie de Courtenay el 13 de junio de 1262, en Seine-Port, Seine-et-Marne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 11 de julio de 1302, en Kortrijk, Flandes Occidental, Bélgica, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Seine-et-Oise, Francia.
